toolbox.gg
Early morning — activate
5:00 AM

Wake up

Get out of bed, drink a glass of water right away. No screens yet — let your body wake up naturally first.

5:05 AM

Get dressed + wash face + brush teeth (10 min)

Get yourself ready for the day — clothes on, face washed, teeth brushed. Clean start = clear head.

5:15 AM

Exercise (5 min)

Push-ups, jumping jacks, or a quick sprint — short and sharp. Every single day. Gets your blood moving and wakes your brain up fast.

5:20 AM

Meditation (5 min)

Sit still, breathe slowly, do nothing. Your heart rate is coming down from exercise — this is the most effective moment to meditate. Bridges movement into calm focus.

5:25 AM

Breakfast (20 min)

Eat a proper breakfast before the day starts. Fuel your brain before the main session.

5:45 AM

Skill building 1 (45 min)

Rotate each day: puzzle solving, logic challenges, problem-solving exercises, playing ROBLOX and studying how games are built, developing in ROBLOX Studio, or learning about the ROBLOX ecosystem.

Mid morning — prime and warm up
6:30 AM

ROBLOX wild card — anything ROBLOX (30 min)

Loose and exploratory — play a game, watch a dev video, tinker in Studio with no goal. Primes your brain for the focused work ahead.

7:00 AM

Skill building 2 (30 min)

Rotate each day: puzzle solving, logic challenges, problem-solving exercises, playing ROBLOX and studying how games are built, developing in ROBLOX Studio, or learning about the ROBLOX ecosystem.

Peak coding — deep work
7:30 AM

Productive free time (25 min)

Use this however you want — relax, get set up, or ease into the next session.

7:55 AM

ROBLOX dev study (25 min)

Lua scripting, building, or game design. One task at a time — write your goal on paper before you start. Stay in Studio.

8:20 AM

Break (10 min)

Stretch, walk around, drink water. No screens — keep your brain in work mode.

8:35 AM

ROBLOX dev study (25 min)

Keep going on your project. Try to ship one small thing each session — a working door, a point system, a new area. Small wins = big motivation.

9:00 AM

Break (10 min)

Snack, fresh air, quick stretch.

9:10 AM

ROBLOX dev study + brunch (50 min)

You're warmed up now — use this longer block for bigger features. Debug, test, write scripts, design game systems. Grab a light brunch during this block at your own pace. Keep the momentum going.

10:00 AM

Productive free time (30 min)

Take a real break — step away from the screen, go outside for a few minutes, stretch, or just do nothing. You've done three solid coding sessions. Rest before the next block.

Late morning — play & learn
10:30 AM

ROBLOX dev study (20 min)

A final coding block before lunch. Work on something small and completable — a script, a UI tweak, a bug fix. End on a win.

10:50 AM

Play ROBLOX (60 min)

Play for fun — but study the games you enjoy. Ask yourself: "How did they build this mechanic?" You're a developer now, so playing is also research.

Midday — lunch & recharge
11:50 AM

Cooking & Lunch (30 min)

Cook your own lunch if you feel like it, or use this as free time. No pressure either way.

12:20 PM

Real break / rest (40 min)

Eat, relax, don't think about code. Your brain consolidates what it learned in the morning while you rest. This isn't wasted time.

Afternoon — more ROBLOX
1:00 PM

ROBLOX dev study (25 min)

Afternoon dev session. Try working on something visual today — a new map area, UI elements, lighting, or animations. Mix it up from the morning.

1:25 PM

Break (10 min)

Stretch, walk, water.

1:35 PM

ROBLOX dev study (40 min)

Keep building. If you get stuck on a bug, write it down on paper and move to a different part of the game — come back to it fresh. Stuck = good. It means you're learning.

Late afternoon — play, create & reflect
2:15 PM

Play ROBLOX (60 min)

Second daily play session. Explore different games, try out things that inspire you, or playtest your own game and make notes about what needs fixing.

3:15 PM

Skill building 3 (45 min)

Rotate each day: puzzle solving, logic challenges, problem-solving exercises, playing ROBLOX and studying how games are built, developing in ROBLOX Studio, or learning about the ROBLOX ecosystem. Keeps your brain sharp before coding begins.

4:00 PM

Daily journal (15 min)

Write: what did I build today? What broke and how did I fix it? What's my goal for tomorrow? One page is enough. This is how developers think.

4:45 PM

Meditation (5 min)

Sit still, breathe, let the day settle. This is your hard stop between work and the rest of the evening. Signals to your brain that coding is done.

4:50 PM

Go outside / move (30 min)

Walk, bike, sit outside — get off screens and get some fresh air. Your brain needs this after a full coding day.

5:20 PM

Productive free time (40 min)

Truly yours — relax, watch something, play, or help around the house. You've earned it.

Evening — wind down
6:00 PM

Dinner + family time (1 hr)

Eat a proper dinner. Spend time with family. Decompress.

7:00 PM

Evening ROBLOX (30 min)

Optional — if you feel like it, hop on for 30 min. Keep it chill and light. Don't start a hard coding task this late.

7:30 PM

Shower + night care (15 min)

Shower, brush teeth, wash your face, handle any night routine. Wind down your body before your mind does.

7:45 PM

Free time (2 hrs 15 min)

Read, stretch, listen to music, or just relax. Wind down at your own pace.

10:00 PM

Lights out

Good night. Tomorrow you build more.
